LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING, WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. PETITIONER CENTRAL VALLEY GENERAL HOSPITAL & ANAHEIM MEMORIAL MEDICAL CENTER'S MOTION FOR A TRIAL ON A CCP 1085 WRIT OF MANDATE AND ASSIGNMENT TO A SINGLE JUDGE IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. IT IS PREMATURE TO DETERMINE THAT THE ISSUES THAT WILL NEED TO BE DECIDED AT THE TIME THE WRIT PROCEEDING IS READY FOR HEARING WILL NECESSARILY REQUIRE ORAL TESTIMONY. A WRIT PROCEEDING IS A LAW AND MOTION MATTER (CRC 3.1103(A)(2)), AND SUCH MATTERS ARE ORDINARILY TO BE DECIDED ON THE PAPERS (CRC 3.1306(A)). THE PARTIES MAY REQUEST THE JUDGE PRESIDING OVER THE WRIT HEARING, ONCE IT IS NOTICED AND BEING BRIEFED, TO ALLOW ORAL TESTIMONY EITHER IN THE BRIEFING OR IN ACCORDANCE WITH CRC 3.1306(B). THAT JUDGE WILL THEN HAVE A DEVELOPED RECORD ON THE BASIS OF WHICH TO DETERMINE IF SUCH TESTIMONY WOULD BE REQUIRED OR HELPFUL ON THE ISSUES THEN PRESENTED AND CAN EXERCISE INFORMED DISCRETION WHETHER AND TO WHAT EXTENT AND BY WHAT PROCEDURE TO ALLOW ANY SUCH TESTIMONY. NO STATEMENT OF DECISION WITH RESPECT TO THIS MOTION IS REQUIRED BY CCP SECTION 632. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce The Stay Previously Ordered By This Court And To Vacate Or Continue The September 29, 2015 Hearing Date
Off Calendar Sep-11-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MICHAEL S. SORGEN, ESQ., Tel No. 510-919-3586 appearing for the Petitioners;
Deputy Attorney General NIMROD P. ELIAS and SUSAN M. CARSON of the Office of the Attorney General, Tel No. 415-703-5841 appearing for Respondents David Maxwell-Jolly Director of the California Department of Health Care Services, California Department of Health Care Services, and Toby Douglas.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling (with the exception of deadlines as the opposition and reply papers). As to RESPONDENTS DAVID MAXWELL-JOLLY DIRECTOR OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH CARE SERVICES, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH CARE SERVICES AND TOBY DOUGLAS' Motion To Enforce The Stay Previously Ordered By This Court And To Vacate Or Continue The September 29, 2015 Hearing Date, the court orders the stay lifted and continues the petition for writ of mandate hearing to October 27, 2015. Opposition papers to be filed no later than October 16, 2015. Reply papers to be filed no later than October 22, 2015. Armstrong v. Exceptional Child Ctr., Inc., 135 S.Ct. 1378 (2015) and Cal. Hosp. Ass'n v. Maxwell-Jolly (2010) 188 Cal.App.4th 559, 569, indicate that adjudication of petitioner's Medicare/Medi-Cal claim cannot occur in federal court and petitioner's remedy is to seek a writ per CCP 1085. There is little chance that the 9th Circuit will reverse Judge Conti's order. Respondent has an onerous task in showing that Judge Conti abused his discretion by dismissing the federal action without prejudice. See Westlands Water Dist. v. United States, 100 F.3d 94, 96 (9th Cir. 1996). Any res judicata or collateral estoppel argument that respondent may make is dubious because the federal court never addressed the underlying merits of the dispute. Lastly, any further delay will prejudice petitioner who is seeking reimbursement. Petitioners' counsel shall prepare and submit a proposed order for signature. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Trila On A Ccp 1085 Writ Of Mandate And Assignment To A Single Judge
Off Calendar May-08-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Gregory D Brown, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Tel. 415.703.5461, for respondents and opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling, which was not contested. PETITIONER CENTRAL VALLEY GENERAL HOSPITAL & ANAHEIM MEMORIAL MEDICAL CENTER's Motion For A Trial On A CCP 1085 Writ Of Mandate And Assignment To A Single Judge is denied without prejudice. It is premature to determine that the issues that will need to be decided at the time the writ proceeding is ready for hearing will necessarily require oral testimony. A writ proceeding is a law and motion matter (CRC 3.1103(a)(2)), and such matters are ordinarily to be decided on the papers (CRC 3.1306(a)). The parties may request the judge presiding over the writ hearing, once it is noticed and being briefed, to allow oral testimony either in the briefing or in accordance with CRC 3.1306(b). That judge will then have a developed record on the basis of which to determine if such testimony would be required or helpful on the issues then presented and can exercise informed discretion whether and to what extent and by what procedure to allow any such testimony. No statement of decision with respect to this motion is required by CCP section 632. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
